channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: A new dating app, Sonder, has a deliberately annoying sign-up process (and it’s working)
item.description: Sonder profiles are completely unstructured, encouraging users to build something that looks like a mood board or a digital collage Think MySpace rather than LinkedIn.

item.title: Lil Finder Guy is the cutest thing Apple has made in its 50-year history — and it has a secret
item.description: People are going gaga over Apple's Lil Finder Guy, who arrived just in time for Apple's 50th Anniversary, but there's more to him than just a cute face.

item.title: Startup funding shatters all records in Q1
item.description: The record quarterly fundraise was largely fueled four mega-deals into OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Waymo. But it also indicates a generally hot market.

item.title: 'API credentials are widely and publicly exposed on the web': Experts scour 10 million web pages and find a shocking amount of security info just lying around
item.description: Researchers examined millions of webpages and found thousands of exposed API credentials, revealing persistent security gaps across cloud services and development environments.

item.title: Anthropic Can’t Cover Up Its Claude Code Leak Fast Enough
Zach Cregger Recruits Second Zach for His Aunt Gladys Spinoff
item.description: 'Weapons' is officially getting a prequel focused on the character played by Oscar winner Amy Madigan, with Zach Shields coming aboard to co-write the screenplay.

item.title: Samsung’s Best OLED TV Is $300 Off
item.description: The gorgeous Samsung S95F QD-OLED is on super sale ahead of the newest models hitting stores.

item.title: If you want a budget TV, this Amazon Fire 4-Series is hard to beat at its record-low price
item.description: For a budget friendly TV, check out the Amazon Fire TV 4-Series 50-inch 4K TV which is even cheaper right now thanks to being on sale at Amazon.

item.title: Can You Run a Computer Without RAM? Surprisingly, Yes—But You’ll Be Miserable
item.description: Shell out for RAM, or try to do without—you're going to have a bad time either way.

item.title: Samsung's new 2026 TVs are live, which means you can score record-low prices on existing models — here are 12 deals I'd buy from $249.99
item.description: Samsung's new 2026 TVs have been released, which means you can score massive discounts on existing models, and I've rounded up the 12 best deals from $249.99.

item.title: The Artemis Moon base project is legally dubious
item.description: With NASA planning to launch four astronauts on Wednesday on its Artemis II mission, the race to return to the Moon is back on. The current mission will see astronauts aboard the Orion capsule travel around the Moon before returning to Earth in 10 days' time. They'll be testing out the hardware and systems that […]

item.title: A Vegas showgirl just sued Taylor Swift—and Swifties are not having it
item.description: The lawsuit claims trademark infringement, but fans are digging up old posts and pushing back. Taylor Swift is facing a lawsuit over her last album, The Life of a Showgirl—from a fellow showgirl.

item.title: Apple releases security fix for older iPhones and iPads to protect against DarkSword attacks
item.description: The security update protects a raft of older iPhones and iPads from attacks linked to leaked hacking tools called DarkSword.

item.title: How Adult Swim Skipped the April Fools’ Nonsense and Dropped a ‘Smiling Friends’ Claymation Horror Short Instead
item.description: Ahead of the 'Smiling Friends' series finale, claymation artist Lee Hardcastle explained how a throwaway gag morphed into an 11-minute avant-garde short.

item.title: 'We're being made to pay for grid access that we aren't using': Public EV charging provider warns of price hikes
item.description: Major EV Charging companies warn of price increases for EV owners on the public charging network, as energy prices continue to soar.

item.title: FDA Approves Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 Pill
item.description: Eli Lilly’s once-daily Foundayo is the second obesity pill to receive FDA approval. It will compete with Novo Nordisk’s pill version of Wegovy.

item.title: Oracle Lays Off Thousands to Offset AI Spending
item.description: Over 500 Oracle workers are being laid off in Kansas City alone.
